episode.title: The City That Started a War

narration: On the windswept coast of western Anatolia lie the ruins of a city the world called a myth. Troy. For three thousand years scholars laughed at the legend of a war fought over a single face. Then a man dug into this hill and found nine cities, stacked one upon another, each ending in fire. The myth was real.

shots.prompt: Sweeping aerial at golden hour over the windswept plain and Aegean coastline of the Troad in western Anatolia, ancient ruined stone walls on a low hill, dramatic clouds and long shadows. Cinematic establishing shot.
The great fortified walls and gate of ancient Troy at dusk, the silhouette of a huge wooden horse standing before them, torches flickering, ominous and legendary. Slow cinematic push-in.
An epic ancient battle on the dusty plain before the city walls, bronze-armored warriors and shields clashing, banners and dust clouds rising in golden light. Wide cinematic chaos.
Extreme close-up of a determined ancient warrior's weathered face, sweat and dust, piercing eyes, bronze helmet, shallow depth of field with battlefield bokeh behind. Intense, heroic.
A 19th-century archaeologist's hands brushing dirt from gleaming ancient golden treasure and jewelry in an excavation trench, warm lantern light, dust motes. Discovery and wonder.
Slow aerial rise revealing layered ancient ruins, the cross-section of nine cities built atop one another over millennia, each level marked by old burn scars. Haunting, epic scale.
